market_context: Markets opened strongly on Iran war de-escalation hopes after Trump indicated U.S. forces could withdraw in 2-3 weeks. Major indices are up 0.6-1.0% with tech leading gains, though oil remains above $98 and energy stocks are declining sharply.

predictions.reasoning: Tech-heavy Nasdaq is leading today's rally with 1.04% gains on Iran peace hopes and positive AI sector momentum from OpenAI funding. Strong premarket momentum and relief rally dynamics support continued upward movement toward $585.

S&P 500 opened up 0.67% on war de-escalation optimism and is building on yesterday's strong gains. Key resistance at 6500 level has been broken, targeting next resistance around $655 level on continued relief rally momentum.

Nike fell 11% premarket after warning of 20% China sales decline this quarter and continued gross margin pressure from tariffs. Q3 earnings showed weak guidance with revenue expected to fall and margins contracting 3-3.75 percentage points.

Energy sector is declining sharply despite oil above $98, with XLE down 3% as crude futures fell 2.5% on peace hopes. War de-escalation narrative is pressuring energy stocks despite elevated commodity prices, targeting further decline to $92.

nCino jumped 23% premarket after beating earnings expectations and raising full-year guidance. Strong fintech momentum and positive earnings surprise should sustain gains, though high premarket move reduces upside potential from current levels.
